_comment: Scenario-staged rout-then-ANNIHILATION demo: a lone low-morale (1) player infantry block of 140 is charged head-on by two 80-man cavalry and BREAKS -- it enters routing (drawn faded) and flees toward its own back edge, past it into the retreat margin. Routing units are still valid combat targets, and the cavalry (8.5 sprint) are faster than the fleeing infantry (4.0 sprint), so pursuit is relentless: they catch it, and the casualties from being run down erode its morale faster than it can recover, so it never rallies. It's eventually ANNIHILATED (soldiers reach zero) rather than escaping or rallying. A second, safe player unit (spearmen at 300,300, full morale, out of detection range) stays in the fight so the battle doesn't declare defeat and freeze the instant the infantry routs. Deterministic via the seed: with this build the infantry routs around tick 140 and is annihilated around tick 440 (~7.3 s at 60 tps), well inside the 300-frame clip. Camera frames the clash and the flee lane above it. No player input needed. (A unit that truly breaks free instead recovers cleanly -- see test/unit/test_morale_recovery_demo_scenario.gd for that case in isolation.)
